title: gymnanthemum theophrastifolium var. richardianum
intro: Gymnanthemum theophrastifolium var. richardianum, also known as Eclipta alba var. richardiana or Eclipta prostrata, is a perennial herb native to Asia, belonging to the aster family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Eclipta prostrata are attributed to its ability to stimulate hair growth, improve liver function, and reduce oxidative stress, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, although its use and efficacy should be evaluated by a healthcare professional.
